question: Is the image a horizontal canvas with a white background, divided into three equal-width columns separated by thin gray vertical lines?
Does the left column depict an inclined plane system with a block (m1) on the slope, a pulley at the top, and a hanging block (m2) connected by a rope?
Is the angle of the inclined plane marked with an arc and arrow, and labeled explicitly as "THETA=30"?
In the left column, are coordinate axes drawn with 'x' along the incline and 'y' perpendicular to it (normal direction)?
Are dashed lines used in the left column to indicate the rope segments, maintaining parallel (incline) and vertical (hanging) relationships?
Does the middle column contain two vertically aligned Free Body Diagrams (FBDs) for m1 (upper) and m2 (lower)?
Does the upper FBD (for m1) display exactly four blue force arrows labeled N, T, F, and mg?
In the upper FBD, is the force N perpendicular to the incline and mg vertically downward?
Does the upper FBD include gray dashed lines decomposing mg into components labeled "mgx" and "mgy"?
Does the lower FBD (for m2) display exactly two collinear blue force arrows labeled T (upward) and mg (downward)?
Does the right column contain three vertically stacked small graphs with aligned x-axes sharing the same time scale?
Are the graphs in the right column labeled with axes 'a', 'v', 'x' (vertical) and 't' (horizontal), including tick marks?
Do the curves in the right column correctly depict: a(t) as a horizontal line, v(t) as a straight line increasing from the origin, and x(t) as an upward-opening parabola?
Is there a vertical dashed line labeled or indicated at time 't1' passing through all three graphs, with small dots at the intersection points?
Does the diagram use only black, gray, and blue colors, with force arrows specifically colored blue?
Are all text labels composed exclusively of Latin letters and numbers, and are they clearly legible?
Are the vector lines, graph curves, and axes smooth and consistent in width without breaks or artifacts?
